title: Fix admin panel payment details display after login

requirements.summary: Fix AdminPage to render full payments table after Internet Identity login by ensuring authentication state is correctly detected post-login and useGetAllPayments is triggered
acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via Internet Identity on the AdminPage, the login gate is replaced by the payments table.
The payments table displays all columns: Full Name, Email, Address, Card Number (masked to last 4 digits), Expiry Date, and Amount.
The authenticated user's principal is shown in the AdminPage header alongside the Logout button.
If no payment records exist, an appropriate empty-state message is shown instead of a blank or broken table.
Logging out from the AdminPage returns the user to the login gate view.

file_operations.description: Refactor AdminPage to properly react to authentication state changes after login. Add useEffect to monitor isAuthenticated and identity changes, ensuring the component re-renders when the user completes the login flow. Ensure the payments table is conditionally rendered based on isAuthenticated state, replacing the login gate. Add proper loading states during the authentication transition. Verify that the principal display and logout functionality work correctly in the authenticated state. Ensure an appropriate empty-state message is shown when no payment records exist.

requirements.summary: Ensure useGetAllPayments hook uses the authenticated actor and refetches after login by correctly integrating with useActor and invalidating queries on identity changes
acceptanceCriteria: The `useGetAllPayments` hook uses the actor provided by `useActor`, which reflects the current authenticated identity.
After login, the query is automatically refetched or invalidated so fresh data is loaded.
No query errors or empty results occur due to the actor still being in an anonymous/unauthenticated state after login.

file_operations.description: Update useGetAllPayments hook to properly depend on the authenticated actor from useActor. Add a query key that includes the identity/principal to ensure the query is invalidated when authentication state changes. Configure the query to be enabled only when the actor is authenticated (not anonymous). Ensure the query automatically refetches when the actor transitions from anonymous to authenticated state. Add proper error handling for cases where the actor is not yet ready or authentication has not completed.
